assets.name: lecture_07_figure_01.png
assets.rationale: None of the candidate frames are useful for lecture-note visuals. This subtitle window contains only the opening Stanford branding sequence: exterior campus shots followed by a title card, with no blackboard mathematics, diagrams, or lecture-room layout to preserve. Candidate 04 is the most finished and legible of the set, but it should still be rejected for the notes.

assets.name: lecture_07_figure_02.png
assets.subtitle_excerpt: It's the fact that energy, which is the same as mass, all you need to do is to write an equation.
assets.caption_hint: It's the fact that energy, which is the same as mass, all you need to do is to write an equation.

assets.name: lecture_07_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 01 is the best frame because it preserves the full rough board sketch most clearly at the moment in question. The grid-like drawing is the main note-worthy content in this window, and here it is more complete and less occluded than in candidate 02, while candidate 03 shifts the diagram farther to the edge. Candidate 04 no longer shows the same sketch as the main content.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: is essentially equal to the potential energy.
assets.caption_hint: Rough lattice sketch on the board

assets.name: lecture_07_figure_04.png
assets.subtitle_excerpt: Now I want to compare some of these equations with black hole physics.
